steps.title: Spot the opening dash
steps.content: The em dash after “ash” signals that a non‑essential phrase begins, so we need a matching dash to close it before the “to craft” phrase.

steps.title: Apply the dash pair
steps.content:
Choice C supplies the closing em dash (material—to) so the phrase “a durable and flexible material” is properly set off, allowing the “to craft baskets” phrase to follow smoothly.

The other three choices either use a colon, semicolon, or period that does not match the opening dash and create a punctuation error or a sentence fragment.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
